Agent: Project Organization Sweep (#7833)

* Agent: Project Organization Sweep

* Initial plan

* refactor(format/printer): move findEnclosingFunctionDeclaration from function-parameter-naming.ts to path-utils.ts

Original location: src/format/src/printer/function-parameter-naming.ts
New location:      src/format/src/printer/path-utils.ts

Rationale:
findEnclosingFunctionDeclaration is a pure AstPath traversal helper — it
delegates entirely to findAncestorNode and has no doc-layout logic.
function-parameter-naming.ts is a doc-layout module (joining declarator
fragments); hosting a path-traversal utility there violated SRP and blurred
the module's purpose. path-utils.ts is the canonical home for AstPath
utilities (already containing safeGetParentNode and findAncestorNode),
making it the natural fit for this function.

All imports/exports updated; boundary tests tightened to assert the function
is NOT in function-parameter-naming.ts; new tests added to path-safety.test.ts
covering null path, immediate parent match, ancestor traversal, and missing
getParentNode. All 286 format-workspace tests pass.

Co-authored-by: henrylkirk <15679826+henrylkirk@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: auto-retry barcode import on transient server parse errors

When createProduct completes, Grocy on home-lab hardware (RPi, NAS)
occasionally returns a partial/unexpected response to the immediate
getProductDetails call, causing a decodingError.

Changes:
- Split importToGrocy into three methods for clarity
- Add 200ms pause before getProductDetails (race condition guard)
- Auto-retry up to 3 times on NetworkError.decodingError/.invalidResponse
  with 400ms back-off between attempts
- Promote guard errors to typed ImportError (cleaner error messages)

Users no longer see the 'could not parse server response' error on
first scan — retries happen transparently in the background.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: handle single-file volume mounts (etc/hostname) (#59)

When containerd sends a volume mount for a single file (e.g.,
/etc/hostname, /etc/hosts, /etc/resolv.conf), the agent creates a
directory for the inline content. crun then fails because it can't
mount a directory onto a file destination.

Fix: when a volume has exactly one inline file and the destination
is a file path (not a directory), point the bind-mount source at the
file inside the volume directory, not the directory itself.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: replace broken Firebase Google redirect flow

GitHub Pages runs on itjey.github.io while Firebase Auth uses
cloak-df1d8.firebaseapp.com, and modern browsers block the
cross-origin storage that signInWithRedirect depends on.

Replace Firebase's popup/redirect helper flow with a direct
Google OAuth start from the app origin using createAuthUri,
then finish auth locally with signInWithCredential(id_token).
This keeps the round-trip on the app origin and avoids the
redirect result being lost on return.

commit.message:
perf: optimize BLAKE3 throughput with fast full-chunk processing

Add a direct-from-input fast path for BLAKE3 that bypasses the
per-block buffer copy when processing complete 1024-byte chunks:

- blake3ProcessFullChunk: processes all 16 blocks of a chunk in a
  tight loop, loading message words directly from the input array
- blake3CompressWithPerm: compression variant with pre-extracted
  permutation pointer, hoisted once per chunk instead of per block
- Batched chunk processing: extracts all unsafe pointers once for
  the entire batch instead of per chunk (5-level closure nesting)
- Eliminates redundant block zeroing in BLAKE3ChunkState.update

The slow path (partial chunks, streaming, finalization) is unchanged.

Benchmark results (1 MB, release, arm64e Apple Silicon):
  BLAKE3:     631 -> 817 MB/s  (+29.4%)
  BLAKE2b:   1249 -> 1255 MB/s (unchanged, already optimal)
  BLAKE2s:    764 ->  766 MB/s (unchanged, already optimal)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: session isolation, agent status tracking, and idle nudge (v0.4.3) (#160)

## Summary

Fixes three bugs and adds two quality-of-life features for agent
coordination.

### Bug Fixes

**1. Cross-session agent/task leaking**
- `GET /agents` now supports `?sessionId=` filter for session-scoped
queries
- `GET /tasks` global scope: active session → session tasks only; no
active session → all historical project tasks (server-derived, no
client-supplied sessionId)
- Added `getBySession()` to `AgentRosterRepository`
- Added DB index on `agent_roster.session_id` (migration 0031)

**2. Agent shows 'idle' while still active**
- Decoupled agent display status from delegation lifecycle —
`notifyParentOfIdle()` no longer marks delegations complete
- Delegation completion now only via `COMPLETE_TASK` or agent exit
- Dedup key cleared on running transition so subsequent idle transitions
properly re-notify

### New Features

**4. Idle task nudge**
- After 30s idle with uncompleted DAG tasks, agent receives a system
reminder
- Nudges once per idle period, cancels on running/exit/terminate
- Timer properly cleaned up in all agent lifecycle paths

**5. Soft idle-role reminder**
- When creating an agent, if idle agents of the same role exist in the
session, lead gets a non-blocking reminder
- Does not prevent agent creation — informational only

### Other
- Version bumped to 0.4.3
- Removed redundant `getByProjectAndSession()` (session implies project)
- Removed duplicate-role creation guard (replaced by soft reminder)

### Tests
- Server: 3,881 tests passing
- Web: 1,494 tests passing
- New tests for idle-role reminder behavior
- Zero TypeScript errors

(`com.github.MinnDevelopment`) to EssentialsX's own Maven repo
